title: F2 Approvals
page_header: F2 Approvals - Final Review and Commitment
grant_remaining_note: Available for new commitments (Total Grant - Total Committed)
all_workplans_have_entries: All workplans already have commitment entries
refresh_note: Click refresh to see latest changes in workplan amounts
uncommitted_desc: Review, edit expenses, reassign grant calls, and commit F1s for final approval
search_placeholder: Search by ERR ID, state, location...
committed_desc: View and filter committed F1s by grant call, donor, cycle, and state
no_committed: No committed F1s found matching the current filters
assign_metadata_description: Assign funding cycle, grant call, MMYY, and grant serial for:
delete_confirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this F1 project submission? This action cannot be undone.
decommit_confirmation: Move this project back to the uncommitted list? You can then delete it from there if needed.
decommit_success: Project moved back to uncommitted list.
explainer_intro: F2 matches F1 work plans to the grant pool: assign grant calls, record commitments, handle community approvals, and view balances by state and by grant.
explainer_tabs_overview: Uncommitted: plans not yet tied to a grant. Committed: plans on a grant, including MOUs and reporting where applicable.
explainer_uncommitted_intro: Each row is a work plan that is not yet committed against a grant. You will typically see identifiers, dates, state and locality, requested amount, community approval status, and action buttons. Filters let you narrow the list by date range and state.
explainer_uncommitted_b1: Select one or more rows and commit when the plan is ready to be locked to its grant call (subject to rules such as community approval).
explainer_uncommitted_b2: Open a row to edit the full project—including expenses—and to assign or change the grant call and related funding metadata.
explainer_uncommitted_b3: Upload community approval documents when they are required before commitment.
explainer_uncommitted_b4: Use row actions to manage the plan in line with your permissions (for example delete from this list when allowed).
explainer_uncommitted_b5: Pagination and sorting help you work through long lists without losing context.
explainer_committed_intro: Each row is a work plan already committed to a grant. Columns add grant name, commitment information, MOU status or links when an MOU exists, and community approval. Search and filters cover grant, state, funding cycle, donor, and dates.
explainer_committed_b1: Open a row to review or adjust details when your role allows, including uploading missing approvals.
explainer_committed_b2: Select rows to create or bundle MOUs with a local partner when that workflow is enabled for you.
explainer_committed_b3: Follow MOU links to F3 when a memorandum has been created.
explainer_committed_b4: De-commit moves a plan back to Uncommitted so you can correct it there before committing again.
explainer_card_allocated: Total distribution allocations in the pool (same meaning as elsewhere in the portal).
explainer_card_transferred: Total activity amounts on grant calls.
explainer_card_committed: Locked-in spend from historical imports plus committed portal project budgets.
explainer_card_pending: Portal project budgets assigned or awaiting vetting.
explainer_card_remaining: Transferred − committed − pending (matches the card subtitle).
explainer_pool_same: These numbers use the same grant-pool logic as F1 and Grant Management.
explainer_by_state: By State — shows how the pool breaks down for each Sudan state: allocated, historical (legacy imported activity), committed and pending portal spend, and what remains. Sort any column; expand the card and use Refresh to reload.
explainer_by_grant: By Grant — one row per grant call with project name plus included, historical, assigned (against that grant), and remaining balances so you can see capacity and pressure at the grant level.
explainer_state_filter: 🔒 If your account is limited to certain states, pool-by-state reflects only those states.
explainer_sources: Pool figures use the same sources as Grant Management (allocations, grants, Mutual Aid Portal projects, historical imports). F2 tables load committed and uncommitted F1 projects from the portal APIs for this workflow.
